mission.title: Install and Configure Redis Operator (Spotahome / OT-CONTAINER-KIT)
mission.description: The Redis Operator by OT-CONTAINER-KIT simplifies the deployment and management of Redis instances on Kubernetes, supporting standalone, cluster, and sentinel modes. It is ideal for users looking for a production-ready solution with built-in monitoring and best practices. Key trade-offs include the need for Kubernetes expertise and potential resource overhead.

steps.title: Add the Helm repository
steps.description:
This step adds the official Redis Operator Helm chart repository to your Helm configuration.

```bash

helm repo add redis-operator https://ot-container-kit.github.io/redis-operator

helm repo update

```

> This adds the Redis Operator Helm repository.
> Updates the local Helm chart repository cache.

steps.title: Install the Custom Resource Definitions (CRDs)
steps.description:
CRDs are necessary for the operator to manage Redis resources effectively.

```bash

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/OT-CONTAINER-KIT/redis-operator/v0.24.0/deploy/crds/redis.opstree.dev_redisclusters_crd.yaml

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/OT-CONTAINER-KIT/redis-operator/v0.24.0/deploy/crds/redis.opstree.dev_redisfailovers_crd.yaml

```

> Installs the RedisCluster CRD.
> Installs the RedisFailover CRD.

steps.title: Install the Redis Operator
steps.description:
This command installs the Redis Operator in the specified namespace.

```bash

helm install redis-operator redis-operator/redis-operator --namespace redis --create-namespace --version 0.24.0

```

> Installs the Redis Operator in the 'redis' namespace.

steps.title: Create a Redis Cluster
steps.description:
This step creates a Redis cluster using a sample configuration.

```bash

kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: redis.opstree.dev/v1
kind: RedisCluster
metadata:
  name: my-redis-cluster
  namespace: redis
spec:
  size: 3
  redis:
    image: redis:7.0.0
EOF

```

> Creates a Redis cluster named 'my-redis-cluster' with 3 replicas.
commands:
kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: redis.opstree.dev/v1
kind: RedisCluster
metadata:
  name: my-redis-cluster
  namespace: redis
spec:
  size: 3
  redis:
    image: redis:7.0.0
EOF

steps.title: Verify the Redis Cluster
steps.description:
This command checks the status of the Redis cluster to ensure it is running correctly.

```bash

kubectl get redisclusters -n redis

```

> Lists all Redis clusters in the 'redis' namespace.

uninstall.title: Uninstall the Redis Operator
uninstall.description:
This step removes the Redis Operator and all associated resources. Data loss may occur if persistent volumes are not managed properly.

```bash

helm uninstall redis-operator --namespace redis

kubectl delete namespace redis

```

> Uninstalls the Redis Operator from the 'redis' namespace.
> Deletes the 'redis' namespace and all resources within it.

upgrade.title: Upgrade the Redis Operator
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure to back up your Redis data. This step includes draining the operator, upgrading, and verifying the installation.

```bash

kubectl drain <node-name> --ignore-daemonsets

helm upgrade redis-operator redis-operator/redis-operator --namespace redis --version 0.24.0

kubectl get pods -n redis

```

> Drains the node where the operator is running.
> Upgrades the Redis Operator to the specified version.
> Verifies that all pods are running after the upgrade.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to create RedisCluster'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The RedisCluster CRD may not be installed correctly.

**Fix:**
Reapply the CRD using the command: kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/OT-CONTAINER-KIT/redis-operator/v0.24.0/deploy/crds/redis.opstree.dev_redisclusters_crd.yaml

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Redis pod is CrashLoopBackOff'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The Redis pod may be failing due to misconfiguration or resource limits.

**Fix:**
Check the pod logs with kubectl logs <pod-name> -n redis and adjust the configuration or resource requests.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'No resources found in redis namespace'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The Redis Operator may not be installed or the namespace may be incorrect.

**Fix:**
Ensure the operator is installed with helm list --namespace redis and check the namespace.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'RedisCluster is not ready'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The Redis cluster may be initializing or facing issues.

**Fix:**
Run kubectl describe rediscluster my-redis-cluster -n redis to check the status and events.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for Redis 7.0, improved monitoring capabilities with redis-exporter.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: If upgrading from v0.18 or v0.19, ensure to review the new Redis configuration options.

resolution.summary:
You should see output similar to: 'NAME              READY   STATUS    RESTARTS   AGE
my-redis-cluster   3/3     Running   0          5m'. This indicates that the Redis cluster is running successfully.
codeSnippets:
kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: redis.opstree.dev/v1
kind: RedisCluster
metadata:
  name: my-redis-cluster
  namespace: redis
spec:
  size: 3
  redis:
    image: redis:7.0.0
EOF

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster running with the necessary tools installed.
